title: Minimum Spanning Trees and Network Design · Adversarial-Input Lab
summary: Connect sites at minimum build cost while explaining redundancy limits. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a minimum-network design and sensitivity brief with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use safe-edge cut arguments to justify minimum-spanning-tree choices.
Run Kruskal with deterministic edge ordering and disjoint-set cycle prevention.
Run Prim with frontier priorities and stale-entry handling.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
